title: agropyron subvillosum
intro: Agropyron subvillosum, also known as the beaked awn grass or beaked spike grass, is a type of wild grass native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a potential treatment for conditions like bronchitis and pneumonia. Agropyron subvillosum can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and stems can be used in herbal remedies, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
